abf_relation: Original inventor of ABF (Ajinomoto Build-up Film) technology in the 1990s. Fujitsu developed the build-up film technology used in IC substrates, which was later licensed to Ajinomoto for manufacturing. Fujitsu is NOT a direct ABF material supplier but holds foundational IP.

business_segments.primary: IT Services and Digital Transformation (Fujitsu Uvance)
business_segments.semiconductor_relevance: Historical - developed ABF build-up film technology; current focus is IT services, not semiconductor materials manufacturing

strengths: Original inventor of ABF technology with foundational IP
Strong R&D capabilities in computing and AI
weaknesses: No longer directly involved in ABF material supply (licensed to Ajinomoto)
IT services focus means semiconductor materials are not core business
Lower margins compared to pure-play semiconductor companies
opportunities: Growing demand for IT services driven by AI and digital transformation
Potential to monetize semiconductor-related IP through licensing
Fujitsu Uvance platform for cross-industry solutions
threats: Intense competition in IT services from global players
ABF technology IP may become less valuable as alternatives emerge
Yen depreciation impacts international revenue conversion

source_quality: T4 - Training knowledge + limited web fetch (fujitsu.com/about page confirmed revenue and employee data)
review_notes: Fujitsu invented ABF technology but does NOT manufacture or supply ABF materials. Ajinomoto is the exclusive supplier. Fujitsu's relevance to ABF supply chain is through historical IP, not current material supply. Recommend downgrading ABF supply chain relevance or marking as IP-only stakeholder.
data_sources: Training knowledge on ABF technology origin
